title: digitaria nuda subsp. schumacheriana
intro: Digitaria nuda subsp. schumacheriana, also known as finger grass, is a type of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and fever, due to its health benefits such as antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Finger grass is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture by herbalists, who may also use it in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
